summary: Ukrainian forces in March 2026 destroyed over 30 Russian armored vehicles, tanks, and artillery using FPV drones and heavy bombers, while the 412th Nemesis brigade reported heavy Russian equipment losses amid intensified assaults. Russian operators used fiber-optic FPV drones "Provod" and "KVN" to destroy a Ukrainian BMP-1 in Kharkiv, and "Yolka" interceptors achieved kinetic kills on enemy UAVs, including simultaneous takedowns by two drones. The "Rubicon" Center conducted 78,499 sorties in March, striking hundreds of targets including tanks, artillery, EW systems, and UAV control points. Ukrainian forces also survived Russian FPV attacks after detecting them with drone detectors, and Russian Ka-52 crews were eliminated by follow-up drone strikes after crash-landing near Nadiivka.
